metadata.title: Reusable Launch Vehicle Maintenance Practices Database
metadata.author: Oscar V. Dbritto
metadata.description: Systematic collection and classification of publicly documented maintenance practices for reusable launch vehicles
sources: SpaceX Falcon 9 press kits and public statements
FAA environmental impact assessments for launch sites
ESA Future Launchers Preparatory Programme documentation
Rocket Lab public filings and statements

maintenance_records.description: Borescope inspection of turbopump turbine blades and combustion chamber lining for erosion, cracking, or thermal damage after each flight
maintenance_records.source: SpaceX public statements; FAA environmental assessments

maintenance_records.description: Static fire test of all nine first-stage engines on the launch pad prior to flight to verify propulsion system health and performance parameters
maintenance_records.source: SpaceX webcast commentary; launch manifest records

maintenance_records.description: Visual and photographic inspection of structural panels for dents, delamination, heat damage, and stress corrosion after recovery
maintenance_records.source: Public imagery analysis; SpaceX press kits

maintenance_records.component: Octaweb heat shield and engine section TPS
maintenance_records.description: Inspection and replacement of ablative thermal protection material on the octaweb heat shield protecting engines during re-entry. Tiles may be replaced if charring exceeds threshold depth
maintenance_records.source: SpaceX public statements; photographic analysis of returned boosters

maintenance_records.description: Inspection of telescoping landing leg mechanisms for buckling, hydraulic seal integrity, and deployment actuator function after landing impact loads
maintenance_records.source: SpaceX press kits; visible landing leg damage in recovery footage

maintenance_records.description: End-to-end avionics functional test including inertial measurement unit calibration, GPS lock verification, flight computer self-test, and telemetry link validation
maintenance_records.source: Standard aerospace practice; FAA launch license requirements

maintenance_records.description: Helium leak testing of all propellant feed lines, valves, and pressure vessels to verify seal integrity after thermal cycling from re-entry
maintenance_records.source: Standard aerospace practice; NASA COPV inspection guidelines

maintenance_records.description: Thermographic and visual inspection of titanium grid fins for thermal fatigue, surface oxidation, and actuator bearing wear after aerodynamic heating during re-entry
maintenance_records.source: Public imagery; SpaceX transition from aluminium to titanium grid fins

maintenance_records.description: Inspection and servicing of hold-down clamps, umbilical connections, and strongback erection mechanisms between launches
maintenance_records.source: FAA environmental impact assessments for LC-39A and SLC-40

maintenance_records.description: Ultrasonic NDT inspection of composite overwrapped pressure vessels (COPVs) for delamination, fiber breakage, or liner cracking. COPVs are life-limited components with defined flight cycle limits
maintenance_records.source: NASA COPV standards; AMOS 2019 anomaly investigation public findings

maintenance_records.description: Borescope and visual inspection of the BE-3PM hydrogen/oxygen engine combustion chamber and nozzle for thermal erosion after suborbital flight profile
maintenance_records.source: Blue Origin public statements; FAA launch license documentation

maintenance_records.component: Crew capsule parachute system (3x drogue + 3x main)
maintenance_records.description: Full replacement of all drogue and main parachutes after each flight. Parachutes are single-use textile systems and are not refurbished
maintenance_records.source: Blue Origin public statements; standard crew vehicle practice

maintenance_records.description: Thermographic inspection of ring fin and wedge fin leading edges for thermal damage from re-entry aerodynamic heating
maintenance_records.source: Blue Origin mission profiles; FAA environmental assessments

maintenance_records.component: Starship ceramic heat shield tiles (~18,000 tiles)
maintenance_records.description: Visual inspection and replacement of damaged or missing ceramic thermal protection tiles on the Starship windward surface. Each tile must be individually checked for cracks, debonding, or gap filler displacement
maintenance_records.source: SpaceX IFT flight footage; public tile inspection imagery; Space Shuttle TPS heritage comparison

maintenance_records.description: Borescope inspection of full-flow staged combustion Raptor engines. Higher chamber pressures and oxygen-rich preburner create more demanding inspection requirements than open-cycle engines
maintenance_records.source: SpaceX public statements on Raptor reliability targets

maintenance_records.description: Inspection of tower catch arms, guide rails, and load-bearing pins after booster catch. Novel recovery system with no heritage — maintenance procedures are being developed in real time
maintenance_records.source: SpaceX IFT-5 catch footage; public engineering analysis

maintenance_records.description: Inspection and replacement of parachute and parafoil systems used for mid-air helicopter catch or ocean splashdown recovery of the Electron first stage
maintenance_records.source: Rocket Lab public statements and SEC filings

maintenance_records.description: Functional testing and inspection of electric pump-fed Rutherford engines after saltwater exposure from ocean recovery. Electric turbopumps have different failure modes than traditional gas-generator turbopumps
maintenance_records.source: Rocket Lab public statements on reuse programme

maintenance_records.description: Planned hot-fire acceptance testing of Prometheus reusable engine between flights. Engine designed for minimum 5 reuses with reduced inter-flight inspection requirements compared to Merlin
maintenance_records.source: ESA Future Launchers Preparatory Programme; ArianeGroup public presentations

maintenance_records.description: Planned ultrasonic NDT inspection of metallic tank structures for fatigue cracking at weld joints and stress concentration points after re-entry thermal and mechanical loads
maintenance_records.source: ESA FLPP documentation; IAC conference papers on Themis demonstrator
